food_subgroup: Set meal with rice, mushroom broth, and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for lion's mane mushroom thick broth rice set meal. FOODON_00002809 (rice dish) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (mixed dishes, rice-based) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for lion's mane mushroom broth rice set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific set meal.

serving.description: 1 set meal (rice + broth + sides + lemon tea)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising approximately 200 g steamed white rice, 150 g lion's mane mushroom thick broth, assorted vegetable sides (~100 g), and one cup of lemon tea (~100 ml), totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: cooked white rice (USDA FoodData Central #168878), lion's mane mushroom cooked (USDA FoodData Central #169403), mixed vegetable sides (HPB Singapore Foodo reference), and lemon tea (USDA FoodData Central #174856); weighted by estimated serving proportions.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: cooked white rice (~36% of total weight), lion's mane mushroom broth (~27%), vegetable sides (~18%), and lemon tea (~18%). Values are expressed per 100 g of the combined set meal. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white rice (cooked) GI ~64 weighted with low-GI mushroom and vegetable components; composite meal GI estimated at 54.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this set meal. Composite GI estimated using the weighted-average method: cooked white rice GI 64 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021) weighted at 36% of carbohydrate contribution; lion's mane mushroom and vegetable components have negligible glycemic contribution (GI ~15 estimated for mushroom/vegetable fraction). Lemon tea with minimal sugar contributes low GI. Weighted composite GI calculated as approximately 54, placing it in the low category (≤55). GL per serving = (54 × 101.75 g carbohydrate in full serving) / 100 = 54.9.

hypertension.guidance: The thick broth component contributes approximately 210 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1155 mg per full set meal), which represents about 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth or consume a smaller portion of the broth.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: The set meal has an estimated composite GI of 54 (low category) and a glycemic load of approximately 54.9 per serving, driven primarily by the white rice component; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size of rice and consider substituting brown rice to further reduce postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and beneficial mushroom bioactives
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) contains beta-glucans and polysaccharides associated with modest LDL-lowering effects; the set meal is low in saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~5 mg per 100 g), making it a generally suitable option for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, provided sodium intake from the broth is moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Rice Set with Lemon Tea is a Singapore restaurant set meal featuring steamed white rice served with a thick, savoury broth made from lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), accompanied by vegetable sides and a house-made iced or hot lemon tea. Lion's mane mushroom is prized in East Asian cuisine for its seafood-like texture, mild umami flavour, and functional health properties including beta-glucan content associated with immune modulation and potential neuroprotective effects. The broth is typically simmered to a thick, velvety consistency using mushroom stock and light seasoning. The rice base provides the primary carbohydrate energy source, while the mushroom broth contributes protein, dietary fibre, and micronutrients including B vitamins and potassium. Per 100 g of the combined set meal, the dish provides approximately 98 kcal, 3.2 g protein, 1.4 g fat, and 18.5 g carbohydrate. The estimated composite glycemic index is 54 (low category), making it a moderate-GI meal suitable for blood glucose management when portion size is controlled. Sodium content from the broth (~210 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for individuals with hypertension. The lemon tea component adds vitamin C and antioxidant flavonoids. This set meal is served at Flavours by Saute (Funan, Singapore) and is positioned as a health-conscious, globally inspired dish drawing on traditional Chinese medicinal food culture.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked white rice, lion's mane mushroom, vegetable sides, and lemon tea, with proportions estimated from typical restaurant set meal composition. GI is estimated using the weighted-average method from University of Sydney GI Database reference values. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation basis.
notes: Nutrient values are per 100 g of the combined set meal (rice + broth + sides + lemon tea), estimated by weighted component analysis. Serving size estimated at 550 g based on typical Singapore restaurant set meal portions. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this mushroom-and-rice combination in the component sources used; lion's mane mushroom is not a significant B12 source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries for the component foods used in this estimation. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as this is a minimally processed plant-based and rice dish with no industrial trans fat sources; confirmed zero by component source review. The lemon tea component is assumed to contain minimal added sugar (~5 g per 100 ml tea); if house-made tea contains higher sugar, carbohydrate and energy values may be higher. Halal status is assumed based on the plant-based and mushroom-dominant composition; restaurant-level halal certification should be verified independently. glycemic_index set to 54 using weighted composite method; gi_category is low (≤55 per Veritas threshold rules). GL per serving calculated as (54 × 101.75) / 100 = 54.9, where 101.75 g is the estimated total carbohydrate in a 550 g serving at 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.
